name: MCP Security Hub
what_it_does: A collection of 36 Docker-based MCP servers that expose 175+ offensive security tools (Nmap, Nuclei, SQLMap, radare2, Ghidra, and more) to AI assistants via natural language for authorized penetration testing and security assessments.
use_cases: Run Nmap network scans through an AI assistant using natural language scan descriptions
Automate web vulnerability scanning with Nuclei templates via conversational commands
Perform binary reverse engineering with radare2 or Ghidra assisted by AI analysis
Conduct OSINT reconnaissance using integrated Shodan, VirusTotal, and threat intelligence tools
Orchestrate multi-tool security assessment workflows with Docker Compose
not_for: Defensive/detection security use cases — this is explicitly an offensive toolset
Unauthorized testing — legal authorization is required for every target
Teams without Docker expertise — setup involves building and orchestrating many containers
Production environments where security tools should not be present
best_when: You are a security professional conducting authorized penetration tests and want AI assistance to orchestrate and interpret results from industry-standard offensive tools.
avoid_when: You need defensive, monitoring, or compliance-oriented security tooling; or you cannot guarantee written authorization for all targets tested.

auth.notes: Most tools require no authentication. Third-party integrations (Shodan, VirusTotal, OTX) require API keys configured as Docker environment variables.

pricing.notes: Open source under the repo's license. Some integrated tools (Shodan, IDA Pro) have their own commercial licensing requirements. Docker compute costs depend on self-hosted infrastructure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Tool errors from underlying security utilities (e.g., Nmap timeouts, connection refused) may not be normalized for agent consumption.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Reconnaissance and scanning tools are generally idempotent (read-only against targets). Exploitation tools are not.
known_agent_gotchas: Legal liability: AI agents can run offensive tools autonomously — strict scope control and authorization verification are critical
Building all 36 Docker images requires significant disk space and build time
Long-running scan tools (Nmap, fuzzing) may exceed MCP timeout windows
Some tools (IDA Pro, commercial scanners) require separate license acquisition
Agent must understand scope boundaries — no built-in target allowlisting to prevent out-of-scope scanning
